blocks.title: Creating Objects

blocks.content:
## Accessing Object Properties

```javascript
let person = { name: 'Alice', age: 25 };

console.log(person.name);   // Alice
console.log(person['age']);  // 25
```

You can use **dot notation** (person.name) or **bracket notation** (person['age']).

blocks.title: Accessing Properties

blocks.content:
## Modifying Properties

You can change object properties after creating them.

blocks.title: Updating Object Properties

questions.question: How do you access the 'name' property from an object?
questions.explanation: Both dot notation (object.name) and bracket notation (object['name']) work for accessing properties.
